hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (49 mg/100 g) and high potassium (317 mg/100 g) yield a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio consistent with DASH dietary principles.
hypertension.guidance: Raw snakehead fish is an excellent protein choice for hypertensive individuals; prepare by steaming or poaching without added salt or high-sodium sauces to preserve its naturally low-sodium, high-potassium nutritional profile.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar content means no postprandial glycaemic impact; high protein (21 g/100 g) supports satiety and lean muscle maintenance.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Snakehead fish is an ideal protein source for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as it contributes no dietary carbohydrate and therefore has no glycaemic impact; avoid battered, sweet-glazed, or sauce-heavy preparations that add carbohydrates.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low total fat (2.5 g/100 g) with low saturated fat (~0.6 g/100 g), high lean protein, and moderate cholesterol (149 mg/100 g) within a low-calorie profile (106 kcal/100 g).
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Snakehead fish is a lean, low-saturated-fat protein source suitable for cardiovascular disease management; steaming or poaching is preferred to avoid added dietary fats, and portion sizes should be managed to keep dietary cholesterol within recommended daily limits.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Snakehead fish provides lean protein and contains omega-3 fatty acids within its fat fraction, but the total fat content is low (2.5 g/100 g), limiting the absolute omega-3 contribution; no confirmed anti-inflammatory micronutrients are present in meaningful quantities.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Snakehead fish is nutritionally neutral for rheumatoid arthritis — it poses no inflammatory risk due to very low saturated fat and negligible sodium, but it does not provide sufficient omega-3 fatty acids alone to confer meaningful anti-inflammatory benefit; steaming or poaching is the preferred preparation to avoid AGE formation from high-heat cooking.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Snakehead fish (Toman), known scientifically as Channa micropeltes, is a large freshwater fish native to Southeast Asia and widely consumed in Singapore, Malaysia, and Indonesia. In its raw state, the fish has a dark brown to black dorsal surface with white to pinkish flesh that is firm, meaty, and low in fat. Per 100 g, raw Toman provides 106 kcal, 21 g of high-quality protein, 2.5 g of total fat (of which approximately 0.6 g is saturated), and 0 g of carbohydrate, making it one of the leanest and most protein-dense freshwater fish available in the region. Sodium is very low at 49 mg/100 g while potassium is high at 317 mg/100 g, yielding an excellent sodium-to-potassium ratio aligned with DASH dietary principles for blood pressure management. Cholesterol is moderate at 149 mg/100 g. The fish is traditionally valued in Singapore and Malaysia for post-surgical and post-partum recovery due to its high protein content and perceived wound-healing properties. It is commonly prepared by steaming with ginger and soy sauce, poaching in clear broth, or pan-frying. Toman is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes (zero carbohydrate, no glycaemic impact), hypertension (low sodium, high potassium), and cardiovascular disease (low saturated fat, lean protein). It is halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. The glycemic index is not applicable as the food contains negligible carbohydrates. Its firm and meaty texture makes it versatile for various culinary applications, often served in soups or as a main protein dish.

notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as snakehead fish is a pure animal protein with no carbohydrate content; confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database. Sugar_g is naturally zero for the same reason. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is an animal-derived food product with no plant fibre. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for snakehead (Toman) fish is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this food. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database for this entry. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient detail beyond the reported fields is not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database for this specific entry. Saturated fat estimated at 0.6 g/100 g based on USDA FoodData Central data for raw snakehead fish (closest approved secondary source) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown for this entry. Glycemic index is set to numeric 0 as a sentinel value per template requirements; gi_category is 'not_applicable' because this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0 g/100 g per HPB) and GI testing is not meaningful for zero-carbohydrate foods. Snakehead fish is traditionally used in Singapore and Malaysia for post-surgical and post-partum recovery. The food is classified as Singaporean/Southeast Asian cuisine and is widely available at wet markets and supermarkets in Singapore.
